parameters.name: blendmethod
parameters.label: Method
parameters.description: The blend method to produce a seamless sequence:

parameters.name: blendfunc
parameters.label: Shape
parameters.description: The blend interpolation shape to use. See Shape in the .

parameters.name: blendbyinput
parameters.label: First Input Specifies Blend Regions
parameters.description: When this is checked on, the first input can be a multi-channel input which specifies blend regions for the remaining inputs into the Join . Channel 1 of input0 is used to blend between input1 & input

parameters.name: blendregion
parameters.label: Region
parameters.description: The size of the blend region.

parameters.name: blendbias
parameters.label: Bias
parameters.description: Which segment to favour when blending: the previous (-1), the next (+1) or neither (0).

parameters.name: match
parameters.label: Match by
parameters.description: Match channels between inputs by index or by name.

parameters.name: step
parameters.label: Step
parameters.description: If set to 1, the next segment will be shifted up or down so that it begins where the last segment ended.

parameters.name: stepscope
parameters.label: Step Scope
parameters.description: The names of channels that use Step.

parameters.name: blendscope
parameters.label: Blend Scope
parameters.description: The names of the channels that should be blended. Other channels will not be blended.

parameters.name: transscopex
parameters.label: Translate X Blend
parameters.description: The names of channels that will be translation-blended. Each string field contains a list of its component channels, such as *tx, *ty and *tz.

parameters.name: transscopey
parameters.label: Translate Y Blend
parameters.description: The names of channels that will be translation-blended. Each string field contains a list of its component channels, such as *tx, *ty and *tz.

parameters.name: transscopez
parameters.label: Translate Z Blend
parameters.description: The names of channels that will be translation-blended. Each string field contains a list of its component channels, such as *tx, *ty and *tz.

parameters.name: quatrot
parameters.label: Quaternion Blend
parameters.description: Use quaternion blending on rotation channels.

parameters.name: shortrot
parameters.label: Shortest Path Rotation Blending
parameters.description: If enabled, compensates.

parameters.name: rotscope
parameters.label: Rotation Scope
parameters.description: Enabled when Shortest Rotation Blending is turned on.

parameters.name: cyclelen
parameters.label: Cycle Length
parameters.description: Enabled when Shortest Rotation Blending is turned on.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page. See .

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).

parameters.name: chops
parameters.label: Chops
parameters.description: Help Not Available.
